questions.explanation: Xcode uses schemes to define build/run settings, and the Run action builds and launches the target on a simulator or device.
questions.question: In Xcode, which feature is primarily used to build and run an app on a connected device or simulator?

questions.explanation: An IDE integrates common development tools (editor, compiler/build system, debugger, etc.) into one workflow.

options.A: Provide a single application that combines editing, building, and debugging tools
options.B: Replace the operating system for development
options.C: Guarantee bug-free code through automatic verification

questions.question: What is the main purpose of an integrated development environment (IDE)?

questions.explanation: UIViewController is the standard controller class for managing a screen’s view hierarchy and lifecycle in UIKit.
questions.question: In UIKit, which class is most commonly used as the base for a screen's controller logic?

questions.explanation: Flip-flops are sequential elements that store state (typically 1 bit) and update on clock edges.

options.A: A way to store 1 bit of state synchronized to a clock
options.B: A combinational logic function with no memory

questions.question: In digital systems, what does a flip-flop primarily provide?

questions.explanation: UI updates must occur on the main thread; in Swift this is done via MainActor or DispatchQueue.main.async.

options.A: Update the label directly from the background thread for best performance
options.B: Dispatch the UI update to the main thread (e.g., MainActor or DispatchQueue.main)
options.C: Disable ARC so the UI can be updated safely
options.D: Use a global variable to bypass thread rules

questions.question: You are writing Swift code that updates a UI label after receiving sensor data from a background task. What is the correct approach?

questions.explanation: SwiftUI updates views automatically when state changes via @State, @ObservedObject/@StateObject, and @Published properties.

options.A: Use a constant let value and rebuild manually
options.B: Use @State or an ObservableObject with @Published and bind the view to it
options.C: Use only static functions to render the view
options.D: Store the value only in UserDefaults and read it once

questions.question: In SwiftUI, which mechanism is most appropriate to keep a view updated when a model value changes over time (e.g., a live sensor reading)?

questions.explanation: Codable with JSONDecoder provides type-safe, maintainable JSON parsing and integrates well with Swift models.

options.A: Use JSONSerialization and cast dictionaries everywhere
options.D: Store the raw JSON string and avoid parsing

questions.question: When consuming a RESTful API that returns JSON, what is the most idiomatic way in Swift to parse the response into strongly typed models?

questions.explanation: High-frequency polling wastes CPU/radio time; push-based or long-polling with backoff reduces overhead while maintaining timely updates.

options.A: Increase polling frequency to 50 ms to reduce latency
options.B: Switch to a push-based approach (e.g., WebSocket/MQTT) or use long-polling with backoff
options.C: Disable TLS to reduce CPU usage
options.D: Move networking to the main thread to simplify scheduling

questions.question: An app uses URLSession to poll an embedded device every 200 ms. After a few minutes, the app becomes sluggish and battery drain increases. Which change best addresses this while keeping responsiveness?

questions.explanation: The Leaks instrument (often used alongside Allocations) helps detect leaked memory and trace allocation backtraces.
questions.question: You suspect a memory leak in an iOS app that communicates with an embedded device. Which Instruments tool is most appropriate to confirm leaked allocations and identify their origin?

questions.explanation: Continuations are the standard way to adapt callback-based APIs to async/await while preserving error handling and structured concurrency.

options.A: Rewrite the entire library in Swift before using it
options.B: Wrap the callback API using withCheckedThrowingContinuation (or withCheckedContinuation) and expose an async function
options.C: Call the Objective-C API only from the main thread to avoid concurrency issues
options.D: Use global variables to store callback results and poll until they change

questions.question: You are integrating a legacy Objective-C library that exposes a callback-based API into a modern Swift async/await codebase. What is the best approach to bridge it safely and cleanly?
